summary: The frontend is a component‑based Angular application that implements the legal deed and document management domain through a layered architecture (presentation, service, and adapter/infrastructure). It leverages Angular, TypeScript, RxJS, Angular Material and a suite of RESTful APIs to deliver capabilities such as deed CRUD, document import/export, workflow execution, authentication, key management and reporting. While the codebase follows standard Angular patterns, the current structure shows moderate separation of concerns, coupling, and cohesion, with a high count of services, adapters, pipes, and components that introduce technical debt and maintenance overhead.

layers: Adapter/Infrastructure Layer (Adapter services, API modules, HTTP interfaces)

capabilities: Create, edit, view and delete deeds
Import and export documents (JSON, PDF, HTML)
Execute workflow actions (archive, change, delete, re‑encryption)
Handle document archiving and custody operations
Perform user authentication and session management
Manage encryption keys and re‑encryption processes
Generate and retrieve reports and analytics
Schedule and monitor background jobs and tasks
Search and filter deed / document data
Provide role‑based access control via guards
functional.bounded_context: Legal Deed & Document Management (Deed Registry) with integrated workflow and archiving

debt_indicators: Very large number of services (131) – risk of God‑service / mixed responsibilities
Big set of adapters (50) – may hide duplicated integration logic
67 pipes – possible over‑fragmentation and low reusability
Components count (128) suggests UI may be overly fine‑grained, increasing maintenance overhead
Potential missing domain‑level abstractions between adapters and services
recommendations: Refactor overly large services into smaller, focused services adhering to SRP
Introduce façade or domain‑specific service layers to decouple UI components from low‑level adapters
Consolidate similar pipes into shared utility pipes or move complex logic to services
Group related components, services, and adapters into feature modules to improve cohesion
Add explicit TypeScript interfaces for service contracts to reduce tight coupling
Review injection points to identify and eliminate unnecessary cross‑module dependencies

overall_assessment: The frontend meets functional requirements and follows a solid Angular foundation, but the high artifact count and moderate structural qualities indicate growing technical debt. Refactoring and better modularization are needed to sustain scalability and maintainability.
top_recommendations: Refactor overly large services into smaller, SRP‑compliant services.
Introduce façade/domain‑specific service layers to isolate UI components from low‑level adapters.
Consolidate and rationalize pipes and move complex transformations to services.

layers: Adapter/Infrastructure Layer (Adapter services, API modules, HTTP interfaces)
